title: Select All Checkbox for Object Views
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want a 'Select All' checkbox on the Object Views panel, so that I can quickly deselect all objects and then pick only the ones I need for a new view.
description:
The Object Views side panel lists checkboxes for each Salesforce object. A 'Select All / Deselect All' checkbox is added at the top of the list to enable bulk toggling.

Behavior:
- When the 'All' view is selected and the panel opens, the select-all checkbox is checked (all objects visible).
- When a saved view is selected, the select-all checkbox reflects whether all objects in that view are checked.
- If any object is unchecked, the select-all checkbox becomes unchecked.
- Toggling the select-all checkbox checks or unchecks all individual object checkboxes.
acceptance_criteria: A 'Select All' checkbox appears at the top of the object list in the views panel
Checking it selects all object checkboxes
Unchecking it deselects all object checkboxes
Select-all is checked when all individual objects are checked
Select-all is unchecked when any individual object is unchecked
Works correctly when switching between 'All' and saved views
ux_changes: Adds a 'Select All / Deselect All' checkbox at the top of the Object Views panel. State syncs with individual object checkboxes in both directions.
implementation_notes: Client-side JavaScript manages the select-all state. The checkbox auto-updates when individual items change and vice versa.
comment_summary: Developer described the implementation plan: select-all reflects view state and syncs with individual checkboxes. Deployed to staging.
